bead.title: Clarify CRM intake target before creating frame/design artifacts
bead.description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<governing>FEAT-011 — DDx preserve outcomes from bounded execution attempts (for example failed required executions or ratchet regressions) are not physics-level conflicts. They terminate the current DDx-managed attempt and hand control back to HELIX for escalation, follow-on beads, or user input.</governing>
</context-digest>

Sparse intake request: "design a CRM".

Interpretation at autonomy=medium: the user wants HELIX to seed governed planning work for a new CRM product design effort.

Conflict detected during artifact traversal: this repository's higher-authority frame artifacts define HELIX itself as the product (`docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md`, `workflows/README.md`). Creating CRM vision/PRD/design artifacts directly in this repo would therefore contradict the current product scope unless the request is intended as one of the following:
- a separate CRM project/repository that should use HELIX
- a HELIX example/demo artifact pack about CRM design
- a reinterpretation into HELIX product work rather than a CRM product

Deterministic next step: obtain the target-project decision before creating downstream CRM frame or design beads.
bead.acceptance:
1. The user explicitly chooses whether the CRM work should live in a separate project/repository, in HELIX as an example/demo artifact set, or be reinterpreted as HELIX product work.
2. The chosen target identifies where governing frame artifacts should live.
3. Follow-on planning beads can then be created without contradicting `docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md` or `workflows/README.md`.
